game_name: Mighty Morphin Power Rangers: The Movie

danger_signals.visual_form: Putty Patrol soldiers and Lord Zedd's monster minions on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat; player must engage or avoid

danger_signals.visual_form: Large unique enemy sprite with distinctive visual design (e.g., Ivan Ooze, season 2 monsters)
danger_signals.meaning: Level climax; high danger requiring special attacks or powered-up state

danger_signals.visual_form: Player character in civilian clothing rather than morphed suit
danger_signals.meaning: Reduced power and defense; weaker attacks available
Imminent defeat; seek healing items or morphing

safety_signals.visual_form: Character transformed into colored Power Ranger suit with helmet
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced combat capabilities; stronger attacks and signature weapon access

safety_signals.visual_form: Thunderbolt-shaped collectible items on screen (small and large variants)
safety_signals.meaning: Morphing meter fuel; enables special attacks and transformation

safety_signals.visual_form: Meter gauge next to life bar reaches full capacity
safety_signals.meaning: Ready to morph or use special attacks; temporary power surge available

safety_signals.visual_form: Ranger signature weapon visible in morphed form (varies by character)
safety_signals.meaning: Access to high-damage special attack capability

faction_indicators.color_identity: Multi-colored (red, blue, yellow, pink, black, white)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ranger suit morphology; character-specific color coding
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Civilian personas represent unmorphed state in same location; transformation sequence shows team unity

faction_indicators.color_identity: Dark/metallic coloration (Zedd in dark robes); Putty Patrol in uniform tan/beige
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Putty Patrol uniform design; monster minions with grotesque asymmetrical features
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Putty Patrollers as basic foot soldiers; season 2 monsters as mid-boss variants; Oozemen as Ivan Ooze's specialized troops
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Monsters appear in levels progressing toward Ivan Ooze final confrontation; Putty Patrol density increases as threat escalates

faction_indicators.color_identity: Dark purple/black with ooze visual effects
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Unique monstrous appearance distinct from Lord Zedd's conventional monsters
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Oozemen spawned from Ivan Ooze; represents corrupted mutation of existing forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears only in final level; represents escalation of conflict beyond TV series mythology

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character displayed in civilian clothing; reduced sprite size or detail compared to morphed form
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Weaker attack animations; slower combat speed; basic kicks and punches only

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character displayed in colored Power Ranger suit with helmet; enhanced sprite detail and armor plating
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stronger kick animations; signature weapon equips; special attack button becomes effective; visual flourish on attacks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gauge next to life bar drains as special attacks used or time passes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player forced to revert to civilian mode if meter empties; attack power reduces; vulnerability increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level 1-5: incremental monster difficulty; Level 6: original creation boss; Level 7: Ivan Ooze final form
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Monster attacks increase in complexity; arena may contain environmental hazards; automatic morphing triggered at boss encounter

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ninja Megazord appearance in ending sequence; visual team formation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Transition to next level or ending cinematic; Rangers shown in unified pose

environmental_storytelling.example: Digitized video of Rangers morphing (based on Season 2 transformation) plays when morphing meter fills; communicates power-up moment narratively
Putty Patrol present throughout; monsters from TV Season 2 in levels 1-5; Ivan Ooze reserved for final level signals narrative climax
Each level begins with Rangers in civilian clothes; must gather power items and morph to become effective; mirrors TV series transformation ritual
Each boss has unique visual design (monsters vs. original creation vs. Ivan Ooze) making progression feel varied and escalating threat
Each Ranger's morphed form associates with character-specific weapon in special attacks; visual identity tied to combat capability

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Patrols level; stands ready or moves in predictable patterns
Charges toward player; performs melee attacks; drops thunderbolt items on defeat
Stands at level end; waits for player approach
Performs multi-hit attack patterns; requires special weapon attack to defeat; may summon weaker enemies
Restricted to weak punch/kick; limited special attack effectiveness
Enhanced kick damage; signature weapon equipped; special attacks deal critical damage to bosses
Presents maximum difficulty; requires sustained special attack strategy; morphing automatic upon encounter

notes: SNES version is primary platformer/beat-em-up hybrid with seven levels. Danger communicated primarily through enemy spawning and boss design escalation. Safety through visual morphing transformation and power-up collection. Faction identity relies on suit coloration and enemy uniform design. World state manifested through character costume state (civilian vs. morphed) and meter depletion mechanics. No exact damage values documented in sources; all assessments qualitative based on game design mechanics.
